Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and high urine protein and ketones, which may indicate renal or metabolic stress. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, but no evidence of infection. Low HbA1c suggests absence of chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function and possible further nephrological evaluation.
